name: Cain
text: Hey, Lyra? Are you still staring at that same page? The sun's almost down.

name: Lyra
text: I'm trying, Cain. I really am. But the letters... they aren't staying in their rows anymore.

name: Cain
text: It’s just a poem. You’ve been at it for an hour. Look, just follow my finger. It’s not that hard if you just focus.

name: Lyra
text: It doesn't work like that for me. When I look too hard, the white of the paper swallows the black ink.

name: Lyra
text: Everything just turns into... yellow clouds. Soft, blurry shapes floating over the desk.

name: Cain
text: Yellow clouds? Lyra, you're just tired. Clouds are in the sky, not in a literature textbook.

name: Lyra
text: Maybe. I just wish you could see the clouds too, sometimes. It’s lonely being the only one in the room with them.

name: Cain
text: Anyway, let's just get through this. If you finish the paragraph, I'll walk you home. Deal?

name: Lyra
text: Deal. I'll try to make the clouds behave for a few more minutes.
